mechanism: Landing zone creation allocates each VNet a unique CIDR from a managed supernet (IPAM_DEFAULT_NETWORK) by querying Azure Resource Graph for existing prefixes and selecting the next free block. No CIDR is assigned manually.
why: Overlapping VNet address spaces block peering at the API level — the only fix is re-addressing, which requires destroying and recreating every resource holding an IP.

related.note: IPAM supplies the address space for each landing zone VNet.
Automated allocation prevents CIDR overlaps that would make peering impossible.

violations: VNet with a manually assigned CIDR — invisible to future IPAM runs.
VNet CIDR outside IPAM_DEFAULT_NETWORK — uniqueness cannot be guaranteed.
Existing CIDR removed from vnetAddressSpace after deployment — resources lose their network reference.
Two landing zones with overlapping address ranges — VNet peering rejected by Azure API.
